title: Lychnis quadrifida
intro: Lychnis quadrifolia, also known as the Four-leafed Catchfly, is a perennial plant in the Caryophyllaceae family, characterized by its distinctive four-leafed foliage and vibrant red flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, with potential health benefits including wound healing, pain relief, and the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Bioactive constituents of Lychnis quadrifolia include flavonoids, saponins, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or dried herb, with the most common preparations being Lychnis quadrifolia tincture, Lychnis quadrifolia tea, and Lychnis quadrifolia capsules, which are used to treat various health conditions.
